title: Outbreak Incidence Law Recovery — extrapolating the post-peak tail
strategies: constant baseline: predict the mean reported incidence (ignores the wave) -> ~0.1
quadratic-in-time + linear covariates: fits the hump in-window but a parabola diverges from the true slow exponential decay when extrapolated to the tail
mechanistic feature family curve = 1/(exp(-a*(x1-tp)) + exp(b*(x1-tp))) with a grid search over the hidden rise rate a, decay rate b and peak week tp, linear coefficients [A,d,e,g] by least squares
alternatives: finer / continuous optimisation of (a,b,tp), sech^2 / Richards / log-normal wave families, ridge-regularised bases, trading complexity penalty against held-out tail error
